as written, step by step, with:

- $file = src/report.ts
- $n = 3

Execution rules:
- Resolve `~/skills/<name>` to ./skills/<name>.mdz in this working directory,
  and read the skill file to resolve any USE target before executing it.
- Every SPAWN MUST be performed as a real Task tool call: exactly one Task call

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- ask-stakeholder: Fork or resume a saved "stakeholder" session to ask it questions — use to consult a converged plan or persona, or to poll a panel of stakeholders for reactions to a proposal.
- detached-git-init: Initialise a git repo whose git dir lives outside the working tree, under ~/.git-detached/<project>. Use when a project must not contain a .git folder (e.g. iCloud/Obsidian synced dirs).
- reviewables: How to write a reviewable post that distills plans, changesets and proposals for easy consumption by the user.
- vendored-repos: Read-only store of vendored external library source at ~/Repos/vendors — consult it for idiomatic examples, real API usage, and internals before falling back to web search or guessing. Also covers adding a repo to the store.
- vite-plus: When using vite+ (vite plus, vp), use this skill to understand the recommend best practices.
- writing-style: Write in Daniel's voice. Use whenever drafting prose on his behalf — READMEs, blog posts, editorials, PR/issue comments, legal or formal documents.
- create-stakeholder: Save THIS session as a "stakeholder session", and adopt the stakeholder role
- simplify: Simplify a module by executing the MDZ simplify skill
- stakeholders: List the stakeholder sessions saved for the current project

---
name: simplify
input: $file, $n
---

# Simplify

Iteratively simplify the code in $file without changing its behaviour: each
iteration diverges along three heuristics and selects, and a fresh-context
judge picks the best iteration at the end.

$heuristics = ["make it more direct", "make it more obvious", "make it smaller"]
$code = the contents of $file
$iterations = []

FOR $i IN [1 .. $n]
  $simplified = USE ~/skills/map-reduce
  WITH
    worker: general
    items: $heuristics
    map: Simplify this code, pushing in one direction: {$item}. Preserve the module's behaviour and public interface exactly; its test suite must still pass. Return the complete revised file, nothing else. Code: {$code}
    reduce: Pick the candidate that most improves the code, or merge two if they compose cleanly: simpler, clearer, behaviour unchanged. Return one file.
  Append $simplified to $iterations
  $code = $simplified
END

$winner = SPAWN general
WITH
  instruction: Compare these versions of the same module and return the best one: the simplest version that is still clear, complete, and behaviour-preserving. The winner need not be the last.
  iterations: $iterations

RETURN $winner

/**
 * Task report module.
 *
 * Formats a list of tasks into a plain-text report: a title header, one line
 * per task, an optional summary section, and a footer with totals. The public
 * surface is `formatReport` and the option/type exports; everything else is
 * internal machinery.
 */

export interface Task {
  title: string;
  done: boolean;
  hours: number;
}

export interface ReportInput {
  title: string;
  tasks: Task[];
}

export interface ReportOptions {
  separator?: string;
  bullet?: string;
  sections?: string[];
  /** Only the "default" theme is registered. */
  theme?: string;
  /** Only "en" messages are provided. */
  locale?: string;
  /** Only plain text output is implemented; the enum anticipates markdown. */
  outputFormat?: "text";
}

const DEFAULT_OPTIONS: Required<ReportOptions> = {
  separator: "=",
  bullet: "-",
  sections: ["header", "tasks", "footer"],
  theme: "default",
  locale: "en",
  outputFormat: "text",
};

const THEMES: Record<string, { doneMarker: string; openMarker: string }> = {
  default: { doneMarker: "[x]", openMarker: "[ ]" },
};

const MESSAGES: Record<string, Record<string, string>> = {
  en: {
    done: "done",
    total: "total",
    doneLabel: "Done",
    openLabel: "Open",
    hoursLabel: "Hours",
  },
};

function resolveTheme(name: string) {
  const theme = THEMES[name];
  if (!theme) throw new Error(`no theme registered under: ${name}`);
  return theme;
}

function resolveMessages(locale: string): Record<string, string> {
  const table = MESSAGES[locale];
  if (!table) throw new Error(`no messages for locale: ${locale}`);
  return table;
}

function message(table: Record<string, string>, key: string): string {
  const value = table[key];
  if (value === undefined) throw new Error(`no message for key: ${key}`);
  return value;
}

const formatHours = (hours: number): string => `${hours}h`;

function renderHeader(input: ReportInput, separator: string): string[] {
  return [input.title, separator.repeat(input.title.length)];
}

function renderTasks(
  input: ReportInput,
  bullet: string,
  theme: { doneMarker: string; openMarker: string },
): string[] {
  return input.tasks.map((task) => {
    const marker = task.done ? theme.doneMarker : theme.openMarker;
    return `${bullet} ${marker} ${task.title} (${formatHours(task.hours)})`;
  });
}

function renderSummary(input: ReportInput, msg: Record<string, string>): string[] {
  const doneCount = input.tasks.filter((t) => t.done).length;
  const openCount = input.tasks.length - doneCount;
  const totalHours = input.tasks.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.hours, 0);
  return [
    `${message(msg, "doneLabel")}: ${doneCount}`,
    `${message(msg, "openLabel")}: ${openCount}`,
    `${message(msg, "hoursLabel")}: ${formatHours(totalHours)}`,
  ];
}

function renderFooter(input: ReportInput, msg: Record<string, string>): string[] {
  const doneCount = input.tasks.filter((t) => t.done).length;
  const totalHours = input.tasks.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.hours, 0);
  return [
    `${doneCount}/${input.tasks.length} ${message(msg, "done")}, ${formatHours(totalHours)} ${message(msg, "total")}`,
  ];
}

const SECTION_RENDERERS: Record<
  string,
  (
    input: ReportInput,
    separator: string,
    bullet: string,
    theme: { doneMarker: string; openMarker: string },
    msg: Record<string, string>,
  ) => string[]
> = {
  header: (input, separator) => renderHeader(input, separator),
  tasks: (input, _separator, bullet, theme) => renderTasks(input, bullet, theme),
  summary: (input, _separator, _bullet, _theme, msg) => renderSummary(input, msg),
  footer: (input, _separator, _bullet, _theme, msg) => renderFooter(input, msg),
};

function resolveSection(name: string) {
  const renderer = SECTION_RENDERERS[name];
  if (!renderer) throw new Error(`no renderer for section: ${name}`);
  return renderer;
}

export function formatReport(input: ReportInput, options?: ReportOptions): string {
  const opts = { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS, ...(options ?? {}) };
  const theme = resolveTheme(opts.theme);
  const msg = resolveMessages(opts.locale);

  const lines = opts.sections.flatMap((section) =>
    resolveSection(section)(input, opts.separator, opts.bullet, theme, msg),
  );

  return lines.join("\n");
}

/**
 * Task report module.
 *
 * Formats a list of tasks into a plain-text report: a title header, one line
 * per task, an optional summary section, and a footer with totals. The public
 * surface is `formatReport` and the option/type exports; everything else is
 * internal machinery.
 */

export interface Task {
  title: string;
  done: boolean;
  hours: number;
}

export interface ReportInput {
  title: string;
  tasks: Task[];
}

export interface ReportOptions {
  separator?: string;
  bullet?: string;
  sections?: string[];
  /** Only the "default" theme is registered. */
  theme?: string;
  /** Only "en" messages are provided. */
  locale?: string;
  /** Only plain text output is implemented; the enum anticipates markdown. */
  outputFormat?: "text";
}

const DEFAULT_OPTIONS: Required<ReportOptions> = {
  separator: "=",
  bullet: "-",
  sections: ["header", "tasks", "footer"],
  theme: "default",
  locale: "en",
  outputFormat: "text",
};

// Only one theme and one locale exist today. Kept as lookup tables (rather
// than provider classes) so adding another is a one-line addition, while the
// "unknown key" errors stay obvious at the call site.
const THEMES: Record<string, { doneMarker: string; openMarker: string }> = {
  default: { doneMarker: "[x]", openMarker: "[ ]" },
};

const MESSAGES: Record<string, Record<string, string>> = {
  en: {
    done: "done",
    total: "total",
    doneLabel: "Done",
    openLabel: "Open",
    hoursLabel: "Hours",
  },
};

function getTheme(name: string) {
  const theme = THEMES[name];
  if (!theme) throw new Error(`no theme registered under: ${name}`);
  return theme;
}

function getMessage(locale: string, key: string): string {
  const table = MESSAGES[locale];
  if (!table) throw new Error(`no messages for locale: ${locale}`);
  const value = table[key];
  if (value === undefined) throw new Error(`no message for key: ${key}`);
  return value;
}

const formatHours = (hours: number) => `${hours}h`;

function renderHeader(input: ReportInput, separator: string): string[] {
  return [input.title, separator.repeat(input.title.length)];
}

function renderTasks(
  input: ReportInput,
  bullet: string,
  theme: { doneMarker: string; openMarker: string },
): string[] {
  return input.tasks.map((task) => {
    const marker = task.done ? theme.doneMarker : theme.openMarker;
    return `${bullet} ${marker} ${task.title} (${formatHours(task.hours)})`;
  });
}

function renderSummary(input: ReportInput, locale: string): string[] {
  const doneCount = input.tasks.filter((t) => t.done).length;
  const openCount = input.tasks.length - doneCount;
  const totalHours = input.tasks.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.hours, 0);
  return [
    `${getMessage(locale, "doneLabel")}: ${doneCount}`,
    `${getMessage(locale, "openLabel")}: ${openCount}`,
    `${getMessage(locale, "hoursLabel")}: ${formatHours(totalHours)}`,
  ];
}

function renderFooter(input: ReportInput, locale: string): string[] {
  const doneCount = input.tasks.filter((t) => t.done).length;
  const totalHours = input.tasks.reduce((sum, t) => sum + t.hours, 0);
  const doneWord = getMessage(locale, "done");
  const totalWord = getMessage(locale, "total");
  return [`${doneCount}/${input.tasks.length} ${doneWord}, ${formatHours(totalHours)} ${totalWord}`];
}

export function formatReport(input: ReportInput, options?: ReportOptions): string {
  const opts = { ...DEFAULT_OPTIONS, ...(options ?? {}) };
  const theme = getTheme(opts.theme);

  const sectionLines: Record<string, () => string[]> = {
    header: () => renderHeader(input, opts.separator),
    tasks: () => renderTasks(input, opts.bullet, theme),
    summary: () => renderSummary(input, opts.locale),
    footer: () => renderFooter(input, opts.locale),
  };

  const lines: string[] = [];
  for (const section of opts.sections) {
    const render = sectionLines[section];
    if (!render) throw new Error(`no renderer for section: ${section}`);
    lines.push(...render());
  }
  return lines.join("\n");
}
```
